author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice
book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.

metadata.author: The Seventh Ecumenical Council. The Second Council of Nice

title: Canon XX.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: That from henceforth, no double monastery shall be erected; and concerning the double monasteries already in existence.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: We decree that from henceforth, no double monastery shall be erected; because this has become an offence and cause of complaint to many.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: In the case of those persons who with the members of their family propose to leave the world and follow the monastic life, let the men go into a monastery for men, and the women into a monastery for women; for this is well-pleasing to God.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: The double monasteries which are already in existence, shall observe the rule of our holy Father Basil, and shall be ordered by his precepts, monks and nuns shall not dwell together in the same monastery, for in thus living together adultery finds its occasion.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: No monk shall have access to a nunnery; nor shall a nun be permitted to enter a monastery for the sake of conversing with anyone therein.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: No monk shall sleep in a monastery for women, nor eat alone with a nun.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: When food is brought by men to the canonesses, let the abbess accompanied by some one of the aged nuns, receive it outside the gates of the women’s monastery.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: When a monk desires to see one of his kinswomen, who may be in the nunnery, let him converse with her in the presence of the abbess, and that in a very few words, and then let him speedily take his departure.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Ancient Epitome of Canon XX.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Monasteries shall not be double, neither shall monks and nuns live in the same building, nor shall they talk together apart.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Moreover if a man takes anything to a canoness, let him wait without and hand it to her, and let him see his relative in the presence of her superior.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: It is evident, as Zonaras remarks, that the double monasteries here referred to are not those in which men and women live together, in one house, which in this canon is not tolerated at all, but those which were situated so close together that it was evident there could easily be an entrance from one to the other, these are allowed under certain cautions by this canon.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: But not only the Greeks but the Latins also often disapproved of such monasteries.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: See decree in Gratian, Pars.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: II., Causa XVIII., Q.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: II., canon xxviij., and Pope Paschal’s letter (Epis.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: X) to Didacus, Abp. of Compostella.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: Despite all this St. Bridget of Sweden again instituted double monasteries in the XVth century, concerning which Thomas Walsingham, a monk of St. Alban’s Abbey, in England, writes that in 1414, King Henry founded three monasteries, of which the third was a Brigittine, professing the rule of St. Augustine, with the additions called by them the Rule of the Saviour.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: “These two convents had one church in common, the nuns lived in the upper part under the roof, the brothers on the ground-floor, and each convent had a separate inclosure; and after profession no one went forth, except by special licence of the Lord Pope.”

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: With regard to the chaplains of nuns, provision is found in Justinian’s Code.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: (Lib. xliv., De Epis. et clericis. )

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: This canon is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: II., Causa XVIII., Q.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy and Ecumenical Seventh Council.
verses.text: II., canon xxj.
